---
title: ""
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    css: styles.css
---

<div class="hero-banner">
  <h1>Conglin (Franklin) Bao</h1>
  <div class="subtitle">M.S.P.H. Biostatistics · Emory University &nbsp;|&nbsp; Incoming Ph.D. Statistics · University of South Carolina</div>
  <div class="tagline">
    I develop machine learning and statistical methods for biomedical and financial data,
    with a focus on predictive modeling, time-series analysis, and high-dimensional inference.
  </div>
</div>

<div class="info-cards">
  <div class="info-card">
    <h4>Education</h4>
    <p>M.S.P.H. Biostatistics, Emory University (2026)<br>B.A. Economics, University of Nottingham Ningbo China (2023)</p>
  </div>
  <div class="info-card">
    <h4>Research Interests</h4>
    <p>Statistical Machine Learning · Time-Series Analysis · High-Dimensional Inference</p>
  </div>
  <div class="info-card">
    <h4>Contact</h4>
    <p>
      <a href="https://github.com/RichardFranklinBAO" target="_blank">GitHub</a><br>
      <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/conglinbao/" target="_blank">LinkedIn</a>
    </p>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="section-title">About Me</div>

I am a graduating master's student in Biostatistics at Emory University and an incoming
Ph.D. student in Statistics at the University of South Carolina (August 2026), where I
will pursue research at the intersection of statistical machine learning, time-series
analysis, and quantitative finance.

My current research spans two domains. At Emory, I work as a Research Assistant under
Professor Jingjing Yang, developing deep learning models (LSTM/BiLSTM) to predict
Alzheimer's disease neuropathology trajectories using longitudinal cohort data from the
ROS/MAP study. I also collaborate with Dr. Ramesh Manyam and Dr. Sheena Warner on a
machine learning pipeline to predict severe maternal morbidity (SMM) using data from
the nuMoM2b cohort, applying ensemble methods including Random Forest, XGBoost, and
LightGBM with class imbalance correction via SMOTE.

Outside of academia, I am interested in quantitative trading, options market
microstructure, and the application of statistical methods to financial time series.

<div class="section-title">News</div>

- **April 2026** — Completing M.S.P.H. in Biostatistics at Emory University
- **August 2026** — Beginning Ph.D. in Statistics at University of South Carolina
- **2025–2026** — RA under Prof. Jingjing Yang (Emory), deep learning for AD neuropathology

<div class="site-footer">
  © 2026 Conglin (Franklin) Bao &nbsp;·&nbsp;
  Built with R Markdown &nbsp;·&nbsp;
  <a href="https://github.com/RichardFranklinBAO" target="_blank">GitHub</a>
</div>
